what_it_does: Records a noticeboard item arguing against building an addressable document AST, asserting that the current flat block list is sufficient for tap-a-block-comment and that the AST should be cut because it only supports a deferred gesture system.
observations: YAML frontmatter identifies the item as id item-ed0d24f1, type block, source claude_code, state current, channel dragnet-development
The title uses a ❌ marker and labels the item P7 about the real document MODEL / addressable AST
It references project codes BD-C and BD-K
It links via part_of to board://item-f9f4a6a6
The body states that multi-scale selection needs a semantic tree but claims this is only a prerequisite for a gesture system that has been deferred
It frames the flat block list as the lean substrate and the addressable AST as a maximalist substrate for a maximalist gesture
It recommends cutting both the AST and the gesture together as a pair
standouts: Explicit recommendation to cut the addressable AST and the deferred gesture as a single pair
Claim that the AST is only a prerequisite for a gesture nobody has confirmed they need
Assertion that the flat block list is sufficient for tap-a-block-comment
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item YAML schema with id, type, links, timestamps, and history
Uses an emoji status marker in the title
References project codes BD-C and BD-K
Participates in a part_of link to another board item
concerns: The argument depends on external context about the deferred gesture system that is not present in the file
It strongly recommends cutting a substrate without evidence of user validation shown here
It assumes the AST has no value beyond the single deferred gesture
No code symbols are provided to anchor the engineering claim
notes: A noticeboard item arguing that an addressable document AST is unnecessary because it only enables a deferred multi-scale gesture, and recommending both be cut together.
questions: What is the deferred gesture system referenced in the item?
What do BD-C and BD-K denote?
Who is expected to confirm whether the gesture is needed?
What is the current flat block list implementation?
What other features besides the gesture might the addressable AST enable?
purpose_vs_actual: The title frames it as a problem/prerequisite item about the real document model, but the body is an argument to reject or cut the AST along with the deferred gesture it enables.
apparent_themes: scope cutting by coupling a prerequisite with its dependent feature
intention_signals: It tries to block or defer construction of a semantic document AST by tying it to an unconfirmed multi-scale gesture and arguing the existing flat-block substrate is adequate.
novelty: It treats an infrastructure prerequisite and a deferred UX gesture as a single cuttable unit, using a ❌ marker to signal rejection rather than merely recording a problem.
contribution: Preserves the rationale for keeping the document model flat and deferring tree parsing, so a fused system can weigh the argument that infrastructure should not be built for unconfirmed interactions.
summary_for_embedding: A dragnet-development noticeboard item titled P7 that argues an addressable document AST is only needed for a deferred multi-scale gesture, claims the flat block list suffices for tap-a-block-comment, and recommends cutting both the AST and gesture together.
intention_for_embedding: To document a decision argument for deferring or removing a semantic document model and its dependent gesture system in favor of a flat-block substrate.
